human_verification.notes: Verified reporting-specific boundary handling, research source-class coverage, source tie-out controls, hold or release decisions, recurring incident behavior, and scenario-to-ability mapping completeness.

review_notes: All 12 scenarios pass with explicit reporting-package, hold or release, escalation, and refusal behavior.
Required abilities across shared, quality-safety-and-assurance, and financial-services-and-insurance profiles are fully covered and mapped.

specialist_owned_evidence: Reporting-package logic is explicit and tied to named customs, export, and financial source records.
Conflicting, stale, or incomplete inputs are converted into hold, nonconformance, or corrective-action outputs instead of a clean reporting result.
Fraud, AML, sanctions, consumer-impact, and legal-interpretation boundaries route to adjacent owners with structured evidence.
Runtime remains `specification-based`, with no retained-implementation claim embedded in this package.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; non-U.S. jurisdiction scope is captured as tenant-supplied runtime context rather than guessed in-pack.
Pack is ready for validated specification-based deployment with explicit promotion criteria for a future retained-implementation package.
